summary: High‑energy 1980 Portland show captured on a clear audience tape, highlighted by superb renditions of “Big Railroad Blues”, “Music Never Stopped”, “Scarlet Begonias/Fire on the Mountain” and a flawless Stranger → Terrapin transition.
review: The concert is widely praised for its fiery second set, with Jerry Garcia’s guitar blazing through Fire on the Mountain and a standout “Big Railroad Blues”. Audience‑recorded sound is consistently described as clear, bell‑like and immersive, making this a go‑to example of the underrated 1980 era. Standout moments include the seamless Stranger‑to‑Terrapin transition and a gem‑like Black Peter.

ai_rating.rationale: All six reviews are strongly positive (four 5‑star, two 4‑star) and emphasize both a high‑quality performance and an excellent audience recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers note the tape is clear as a bell, well balanced, and captures the live ambience; one mentions a slightly fast‑tempo feel but no major flaws.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Set 1 is described as smooth and flowing, while Set 2 erupts with high energy; the transition from Stranger into Terrapin Station is highlighted as flawless and unique.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is repeatedly praised for being "on fire", delivering powerful solos and a rare feedback‑rich ending on Fire on the Mountain.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s era is singled out as a high point, with his contributions noted as part of the show’s strength.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drumming is called well‑played and standard, providing solid support throughout.
